commands.review_summary:
This PR replaces Chutes OAuth token exchange and refresh error-path `response.text()` reads with an 8 KiB bounded `readResponseTextLimited` call and adds focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +78. Total +80 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main source has both Chutes token error branches calling `response.text()`, and the PR adds a focused oversized-stream repro shape; I did not execute tests because this cleanup review is read-only.

target.title: fix(chutes): bound OAuth token error response reads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Chutes OAuth token exchange or refresh failures could trigger unbounded `response.text()` reads on hostile or misconfigured token endpoints, risking gateway memory pressure during auth setup or token refresh.

## Why This Change Was Made

Chutes OAuth already bounds successful JSON token responses via `readProviderJsonResponse`, but HTTP error paths still called `response.text()` directly. This aligns error handling with the existing MiniMax OAuth pattern by using `readResponseTextLimited` with an 8 KiB cap before surfacing the provider error snippet.

## User Impact

Operators configuring or refreshing Chutes OAuth credentials get the same actionable error messages on token failures, without the gateway loading arbitrarily large error bodies into memory.

## Evidence

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/chutes-oauth.flow.test.ts
```

Added regression tests that return oversized error bodies through a tracked `ReadableStream`, spy on `response.text()` to ensure it is never called, and verify the stream is canceled after the bounded read.

AI-assisted: implementation and tests were drafted with Cursor agent assistance; validation was run locally with the command above.
target.author: pick-cat
